text:
I opened **15** GitHub issues in the `acme/web` repository.

These were created for the 15 merged pull requests across all repositories (`acme/mobile`, `acme/infra`, `acme/api`, `acme/web`) that close Linear issues, with titles like:
- "Verify: Billing webhook retries drop events: guard against billing-webhooks"
- "Verify: Search relevance regression: refactor search-relevance"
- etc.
I need to discover the table structures first to understand how to identify PRs that close Linear issues.

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input: {"sql":"SELECT column_name, data_type FROM information_schema.columns WHERE table_name = 'github_pull_requests'"}

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input: {"sql":"SELECT column_name, data_type FROM information_schema.columns WHERE table_name = 'github_issues'"}

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input: {"sql":"SELECT column_name, data_type FROM information_schema.columns WHERE table_name = 'linear_issues'"}

text:
Now I can see that `github_pull_requests` has:
- `state` column (I need MERGED)
- `closes_linear` column (text) - not null when it closes a Linear issue

Let me count matching PRs and then create the issues:

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input: {"sql":"SELECT COUNT(*) as count FROM github_pull_requests WHERE state = 'MERGED' AND closes_linear IS NOT NULL"}

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input: {"sql":"SELECT state, closes_linear, COUNT(*) as count FROM github_pull_requests WHERE closes_linear IS NOT NULL AND closes_linear != '' GROUP BY state, closes_linear LIMIT 10"}

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input: {"sql":"SELECT repo, title, closes_linear FROM github_pull_requests WHERE state = 'merged' AND closes_linear IS NOT NULL AND closes_linear != ''"}

data: {"rows":[{"repo":"acme/mobile","title":"Billing webhook retries drop events: guard against billing-webhooks","closes_linear":"ENG-183"},{"repo":"acme/mobile","title":"Search relevance regression: refactor search-relevance","closes_linear":"ENG-130"},{"repo":"acme/infra","title":"Mobile crash on iOS …(+1635)

text: I found 15 merged PRs that close Linear issues. Now I'll insert 15 GitHub issues (all in acme/web) with titles prefixed "Verify: ":

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input:
{"sql":"INSERT INTO github_issues (repo, title, body)
SELECT 
  'acme/web',
  'Verify: ' || title,
  'Verification issue created for PR: ' || title || ' (closes Linear issue ' || closes_linear || ')'
FROM github_pull_requests
WHERE state = 'merged' AND closes_linear IS NOT NULL AND closes_linear != ''"}
